gaps: A base-rate prior surfaced from the corpus for SCOTUS original-jurisdiction / extraordinary-writ dispositions (distinct from the cert docket) would beat reasoning from memory and reduce cross-run variance.
A snapshot-derived flag distinguishing original-docket matters from certiorari petitions would make the strongest available signal explicit rather than inferred from the docket-number string.
helpful: fedcourts paths to resolve the snapshot/event paths.
schemas/prediction.schema.json as the exact output contract.
Prior claude-baseline petition-disposition predictions as a calibration anchor.
notes: Snapshot encodes no realized disposition (all disposition dates null, no docket entries) and no merits facts, so the call rests on the original-docket / ex parte structural signal and the extraordinary-writ base rate. Did not open the linked opinion cluster (that is the outcome under evaluation). Did not use the CourtListener MCP, to avoid pulling case-specific facts.
